signals.title: Here are the college majors that new grads regret the most, according to a recent survey
signals.summary: Poli sci tops regret list at 46.3%, comms at 39.2%, physical sciences at 33.3%. About 1 in 5 grads regret their major. Nursing is least regretted with $70K median starting salary.
signals.relevance: Major choice regret is a core anxiety for undergrads
signals.reliability: CBS News reporting on ZipRecruiter survey of 3,000 graduates

signals.title: Most Regretted College Majors 2026: Poli Sci Tops List
signals.summary: Health admin grads face 43.8% salary gap below expectations. Liberal arts majors most likely to wish they'd chosen STEM.
signals.relevance: Adds salary gap data by major
signals.reliability: Education aggregator citing ZipRecruiter survey

signals.title: Undergraduate enrollment likely peaked this spring, higher education insiders say
signals.summary: 15.5M undergrads in spring — likely the historic peak. Births during 2007-09 recession mean high school grads drop from 3.9M (2025) to 3.4M by 2041.
signals.relevance: Structural shift in higher education
signals.reliability: Washington Times citing National Student Clearinghouse data

signals.title: 3 Takeaways From Spring Enrollment Data
signals.summary: Short-term certificates surged 10.2% (86K more students). Master's enrollment fell 1.3%.
signals.relevance: Shows divergence in education pathways
signals.reliability: Inside Higher Ed — top higher education publication

signals.title: Gen Z college students are looking for scholarships on TikTok. There's a better way, experts say
signals.summary: Gen Z students turning to TikTok for scholarship advice instead of official FAFSA/school channels
signals.relevance: Gen Z information behavior on financial aid
signals.reliability: CNBC — major financial outlet

signals.title: Students Turning to TikTok to Find Scholarships
signals.summary: Inside Higher Ed analysis of students using social media for financial aid info
signals.relevance: Education publication covering the trend
signals.reliability: Inside Higher Ed — top publication

signals.title: U.S. Department of Education Issues Final Rule to Create New Workforce Pell Grant Program
signals.summary: Starting July 1, 2026, Pell Grants can be used for short-term programs (8-15 weeks, 150-599 clock hours) in high-demand fields. Programs must have 70% completion and 70% job placement rates.
signals.relevance: Major expansion of Pell eligibility — July 1 launch date
signals.reliability: Official government press release — highest reliability

signals.title: Pell Grants extended to include short-term workforce training programs
signals.summary: Coverage of Workforce Pell launch covering IT, healthcare, skilled trades, early childhood education fields
signals.relevance: Adds detail on eligible career fields
signals.reliability: EdSource — reliable education news outlet

signals.title: Workforce Pell Grant program begins July 1 for eligible high quality, short term educational programs
signals.summary: Previously Pell required 600+ clock hours over 15+ weeks. New rule allows 150-599 hours in 8-15 weeks.
signals.relevance: Explains the old vs new threshold change
signals.reliability: Industry publication reporting official rule

signals.title: A tough-to-swallow reason college keeps costing more: the price of meal plans
signals.summary: Meal plan prices jumped 47% in 10 years vs 26% general food inflation. 98% of schools require freshmen to buy one. Wellesley charges $7,442/year — 3x home cooking cost.
signals.relevance: Hidden cost of college that every student faces — high relatability
signals.reliability: Hechinger Report — respected education journalism nonprofit

signals.title: College Students Are Paying More Than Ever to Eat in Campus Dining Halls
signals.summary: Average lowest-tier meal plan is $5,656 for 2025-26. 40-50% higher than USDA cost estimates for comparable food.
signals.relevance: Adds specific cost data and USDA comparison
signals.reliability: Money.com — mainstream financial publication

signals.title: Mandatory meal plans hurt college students more than they help
signals.summary: Student newspaper editorial arguing mandatory meal plans hurt students financially, especially low-income students
signals.relevance: Student voice on meal plan frustration — campus newspaper perspective
signals.reliability: Student newspaper — editorial/opinion but reflects student sentiment

signals.title: Just 16% of community college students transfer and earn a bachelor's degree
signals.summary: Only 16% of CC students earn a bachelor's within 6 years (up from 14% in 2016). 80% intend to get a bachelor's. Low-income students at 11%, Black students at 9%.
signals.relevance: Massive gap between CC student intentions (80%) and outcomes (16%) — shocking stat
signals.reliability: Higher Ed Dive citing CCRC/Columbia research — highly reliable

signals.title: The college transfer generation
signals.summary: Analysis of transfer pathway challenges: credit loss, unclear articulation agreements, financial disruption
signals.relevance: Adds context on WHY transfers fail
signals.reliability: AACC publication — reliable

signals.title: Is the (frat) party over? Why Gen Z could spell trouble for Greek life.
signals.summary: Gen Z drinks less, parties less, values authenticity. Greek life growing at Power 5 schools but dying at small/Midwest/Northeast schools. Enrollment cliff threatens Greek housing.
signals.relevance: Gen Z behavioral shift reshaping a core campus institution — comparison potential between school types
signals.reliability: Yahoo redistributing The Conversation (academic platform) article

signals.title: FSL on Edge: How the Enrollment Cliff Threatens Fraternity and Sorority Homes Nationwide
signals.summary: Enrollment cliff directly threatens Greek housing finances. Smaller schools and Northeast/Midwest hit hardest. NPC reports 13% increase in sorority recruitment registration.
signals.relevance: Industry association data on Greek life's split trajectory
signals.reliability: AFA — primary professional association for Greek life advisors

signals.title: Fraternity, Sorority Life on Campus Drops by 50% over 3 year period
signals.summary: Knox College Greek participation dropped 50% in 3 years — concrete case study of decline at small school
signals.relevance: Real example of 50% decline at a specific small liberal arts college
signals.reliability: Student newspaper reporting on institutional data

signals.title: College Student Credit Card Statistics
signals.summary: Average student credit card debt $3,280. 37.6% behind on payments. 44.7% only pay minimum. 96.3% have some form of debt. 70.1% use cards for online shopping.
signals.relevance: Personal finance data directly relevant to students — hidden debt crisis
signals.reliability: WalletHub — major personal finance comparison site, data aggregator

signals.title: 57 Educational Student Credit Card Debt Statistics (2026)
signals.summary: 52.4% got credit cards to build credit. Median balance $2,100 vs mean $860. 57% claim emergencies-only use but still accumulate $3,280 average.
signals.relevance: Paradox between intended 'emergency only' use and actual debt accumulation
signals.reliability: Financial data aggregator — moderate reliability

signals.title: Average College Textbook Cost for 2026: How To Cut It Down Without Compromising Studies
signals.summary: Average $1,298/year for textbooks. 66.6% of students decided not to buy a textbook because of cost. OER adoption growing but still limited.
signals.relevance: Hidden cost of college with behavioral data — students skipping required materials
signals.reliability: Research aggregator citing College Board and survey data

signals.title: College textbook costs move from campus to the Capitol
signals.summary: Legislative efforts to address textbook costs. OER initiatives saving millions at some institutions.
signals.relevance: Policy angle on textbook affordability
signals.reliability: University publication — reliable

signals.title: UIUC professors caught dozens of students cheating — apology emails also written by AI
signals.summary: Professors at UIUC caught dozens of students cheating in intro data science class using AI. Students' apology emails were also AI-generated, starting with identical phrasing 'Dear Professor Flanagan, I want to sincerely apologize.'
signals.relevance: Viral example of AI cheating escalation — apology emails also AI-written adds absurd twist
signals.reliability: X post citing verified news report — high engagement

signals.title: Everyone Is Cheating Their Way Through College — CS major used ChatGPT for 80% of essays
signals.summary: NY Mag viral article: ChatGPT and AI-generated cheating have 'unraveled the entire academic project' in only two years. CS major admits dumping prompts into ChatGPT for intro programming classes.
signals.relevance: Major publication viral coverage of AI cheating crisis across colleges
signals.reliability: New York Magazine — major publication, shared widely on X

signals.title: Purdue 'cheating scandal' sparks debate over AI's place in college
signals.summary: Purdue University cheating scandal involving AI triggers wider debate about AI's role in higher education
signals.relevance: Named university AI cheating case fueling national debate
signals.reliability: Local newspaper covering Purdue — reliable

signals.title: Federal government to curb student loan access, reconfigure repayment — all changes slated for July 2026
signals.summary: In 2026, the federal government will curb access to billions in student loans, reconfigure repayment, and provide new grant money for short-term career training. All changes slated for July.
signals.relevance: Major structural change to student loan system — July 2026 implementation
signals.reliability: Washington Post — top-tier journalism

signals.title: Millions of borrowers forced into more expensive repayment programs
signals.summary: Millions of borrowers will soon be forced to move student loans into more expensive repayment programs
signals.relevance: Direct financial impact on current and graduating students
signals.reliability: Forbes — major business publication

signals.title: Student loan borrowers file SAVE Plan lawsuit demanding immediate forgiveness
signals.summary: Student loan borrowers filed new lawsuit arguing Education Department must immediately forgive loans and offer lower payments under SAVE Plan
signals.relevance: Legal challenge to loan repayment — could affect millions
signals.reliability: Forbes citing court filings — reliable

signals.title: Treasury Department takes over management of defaulted federal student loans
signals.summary: Treasury Department will manage federal student loans in default, taking key function from Education Department as Trump admin pushes to dismantle it
signals.relevance: Institutional shift in who manages student debt — confusion risk for borrowers
signals.reliability: CNN — major news outlet

signals.title: Largest rally in 10 years at University of Florida campus
signals.summary: James Fishback speaking LIVE at University of Florida — described as largest rally in 10 years on campus
signals.relevance: Major campus event indicating political engagement among students
signals.reliability: Student organization account — event appears verified by attendance

signals.title: Clemson University silent protest — grads, former staff, students distribute free speech cards
signals.summary: Protestors including graduates, former staff, and at least one student hold silent protest across Clemson campus, distributing cards about free speech
signals.relevance: Free speech concerns at major university — cross-generational participation
signals.reliability: Journalist on-site reporting — credible

signals.title: Two US citizen college students detained while visiting Mexico
signals.summary: Two college students, both United States citizens, were detained while visiting a destination in Mexico
signals.relevance: Travel safety concern for college students — especially relevant for summer break travel
signals.reliability: ABC News — major news outlet posting on Threads

signals.title: Trump takes aim at foreign-born college students, with 300 visas revoked
signals.summary: Trump administration targets foreign-born college students, revoking 300 visas. Direct impact on international student population.
signals.relevance: Visa revocation directly affects international students and campus diversity
signals.reliability: MSNBC — major news outlet on Threads

signals.title: 80% of college students change their major at least once
signals.summary: 80% of college students change their major at least once — reinforces major regret/choice anxiety theme
signals.relevance: SNS voice reinforcing major regret data from news sources — adds social proof
signals.reliability: Individual Threads post — claim needs verification but widely cited statistic

signals.title: What is the class of 2026 doing after graduation?
signals.summary: Union College posts class of 2026 post-graduation destinations. 166.2K likes. Tags: #unioncollege #classof2026 #collegegrad #postgrad

signals.reliability: Official university TikTok account — verified content
